---
title: "Cross-platform overview — Projects (canonical fact)"
subtitle: "How conversations link to native projects/spaces/cwd as reported by each platform"
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "Setup — paths, DuckDB connection, helpers"

import sys
from pathlib import Path

_this = Path.cwd()
_project_root = _this if (_this / "src").exists() else _this.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_project_root))

import pandas as pd
import duckdb
from itables import init_notebook_mode
init_notebook_mode(all_interactive=False)

from src.parsers.quarto_helpers import (
    fmt_pct, fmt_int, show_df, show_md, plotly_bar,
    has_col, has_view, table_count, setup_unified_views,
)

UNIFIED_DIR = _project_root / "data" / "unified"
MUTED       = "#D1D5DB"

SOURCE_COLORS = {
    "chatgpt":     "#74AA9C",
    "claude_ai":   "#CC785C",
    "perplexity":  "#20808D",
    "qwen":        "#615CED",
    "deepseek":    "#3B82F6",
    "gemini":      "#4285F4",
    "notebooklm":  "#F4B400",
    "claude_code": "#7C3AED",
    "codex":       "#374151",
    "gemini_cli":  "#1A56DB",
}

SOURCE_LABEL = {
    "chatgpt":     "ChatGPT",
    "claude_ai":   "Claude.ai",
    "perplexity":  "Perplexity",
    "qwen":        "Qwen",
    "deepseek":    "DeepSeek",
    "gemini":      "Gemini",
    "notebooklm":  "NotebookLM",
    "claude_code": "Claude Code",
    "codex":       "Codex",
    "gemini_cli":  "Gemini CLI",
}

# Classification: which platforms expose a project concept natively
PROJECT_NATIVE = {"chatgpt", "claude_ai", "qwen", "perplexity"}
PROJECT_CLI    = {"claude_code", "codex", "gemini_cli"}
PROJECT_NONE   = {"deepseek", "gemini", "notebooklm"}

con = duckdb.connect()
_setup = setup_unified_views(con, UNIFIED_DIR, sources_filter=None)
```

## About this document

The **canonical fact** of chat-to-project mapping, as reported by each
platform. This notebook covers **plane 1** only — what the source itself
exposes via API/raw payload:

- `conversations.project_id` — stable native ID (when the platform has one)
- `conversations.project`    — textual name/path (free-form; CLIs put `cwd` here)
- `project_metadata.parquet` — per-project metadata (Claude.ai system prompts, Qwen)

Inference, semantic normalisation, NLP-matched orphans and path grouping
(e.g. `/qda` and `/qualia-coding` → same canonical project) live in the
**consumer** project — not here. This notebook is descriptive of the raw
fact only.

Three buckets, by platform behaviour:

| Bucket | Platforms | What `project*` means |
|---|---|---|
| **Native project**       | ChatGPT, Claude.ai, Qwen, Perplexity (spaces) | Manual user grouping inside the platform |
| **CLI working directory**| Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI                | `cwd` of the session (file path) |
| **No concept**           | DeepSeek, Gemini, NotebookLM                  | Always NULL by design |

## 1. Coverage by platform

```{python}
#| label: coverage-by-platform

cov = con.execute("""
SELECT
    source,
    COUNT(*)                                      AS total_convs,
    COUNT(project_id)                             AS with_project_id,
    COUNT(project)                                AS with_project_text,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN project_id IS NOT NULL
               OR project    IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS with_any
FROM conversations
GROUP BY source
ORDER BY source
""").fetchdf()

cov["bucket"] = cov["source"].map(
    lambda s: "native" if s in PROJECT_NATIVE
    else "cli" if s in PROJECT_CLI
    else "none-by-design"
)
cov["pct_any"] = (cov["with_any"] / cov["total_convs"] * 100).round(1)
cov["label"]   = cov["source"].map(SOURCE_LABEL)

display_df = cov[[
    "label", "bucket", "total_convs",
    "with_project_id", "with_project_text", "with_any", "pct_any"
]].rename(columns={
    "label":             "Source",
    "bucket":            "Bucket",
    "total_convs":       "Total convs",
    "with_project_id":   "with project_id",
    "with_project_text": "with project (text)",
    "with_any":          "with any",
    "pct_any":           "% any",
})

show_df(display_df)
```

```{python}
#| label: coverage-stacked-chart

import plotly.graph_objects as go

cov_sorted = cov.sort_values("with_any", ascending=False)
orphans = cov_sorted["total_convs"] - cov_sorted["with_any"]

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=cov_sorted["label"],
    y=cov_sorted["with_any"],
    name="With project (any form)",
    marker_color="#3B82F6",
    text=cov_sorted["with_any"].apply(fmt_int),
    textposition="inside",
))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=cov_sorted["label"],
    y=orphans,
    name="Without project",
    marker_color=MUTED,
    text=orphans.apply(fmt_int),
    textposition="inside",
))
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Conversations with vs without a project link, per source",
    height=420,
    template="plotly_white",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02),
    xaxis_title=None,
    yaxis_title="conversations",
)
fig.show()
```

## 2. Native projects (plane 1: as reported by the platform)

Top projects per platform that expose the concept natively. Names are
**raw** — exactly as the platform stores them. Aliasing/normalisation is
*not* part of this view.

### ChatGPT

```{python}
#| label: chatgpt-top-projects

if "chatgpt" in cov["source"].values:
    df = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        project_id,
        project,
        COUNT(*)            AS n_convs,
        MIN(created_at)     AS first_conv,
        MAX(created_at)     AS last_conv
    FROM conversations
    WHERE source = 'chatgpt' AND project_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY project_id, project
    ORDER BY n_convs DESC
    LIMIT 20
    """).fetchdf()
    total_chatgpt_projs = con.execute(
        "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT project_id) FROM conversations "
        "WHERE source='chatgpt' AND project_id IS NOT NULL"
    ).fetchone()[0]
    if not df.empty:
        df["first_conv"] = pd.to_datetime(df["first_conv"], utc=True).dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        df["last_conv"]  = pd.to_datetime(df["last_conv"],  utc=True).dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        show_df(df.rename(columns={
            "project_id": "Project ID",
            "project":    "Name (raw)",
            "n_convs":    "Convs",
            "first_conv": "First",
            "last_conv":  "Last",
        }))
        top_n = df.head(15)
        fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
            y=top_n["project"], x=top_n["n_convs"],
            orientation="h",
            marker_color=SOURCE_COLORS["chatgpt"],
            text=top_n["n_convs"].apply(fmt_int),
            textposition="outside",
        ))
        fig.update_layout(
            title=f"ChatGPT — top 15 native projects (of {len(df)} shown / {total_chatgpt_projs} total)",
            height=520, template="plotly_white",
            yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"), margin=dict(l=200),
        )
        fig.show()
```

### Claude.ai

```{python}
#| label: claude-ai-top-projects

if "claude_ai" in cov["source"].values:
    df = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        project_id,
        project,
        COUNT(*)            AS n_convs,
        MIN(created_at)     AS first_conv,
        MAX(created_at)     AS last_conv
    FROM conversations
    WHERE source = 'claude_ai' AND project_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY project_id, project
    ORDER BY n_convs DESC
    LIMIT 20
    """).fetchdf()
    total_claude_projs = con.execute(
        "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT project_id) FROM conversations "
        "WHERE source='claude_ai' AND project_id IS NOT NULL"
    ).fetchone()[0]
    if not df.empty:
        df["first_conv"] = pd.to_datetime(df["first_conv"], utc=True).dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        df["last_conv"]  = pd.to_datetime(df["last_conv"],  utc=True).dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        show_df(df.rename(columns={
            "project_id": "Project ID",
            "project":    "Name (raw)",
            "n_convs":    "Convs",
            "first_conv": "First",
            "last_conv":  "Last",
        }))
        top_n = df.head(15)
        fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
            y=top_n["project"], x=top_n["n_convs"],
            orientation="h",
            marker_color=SOURCE_COLORS["claude_ai"],
            text=top_n["n_convs"].apply(fmt_int),
            textposition="outside",
        ))
        fig.update_layout(
            title=f"Claude.ai — top 15 native projects (of {len(df)} shown / {total_claude_projs} total)",
            height=520, template="plotly_white",
            yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"), margin=dict(l=250),
        )
        fig.show()
```

### Qwen

```{python}
#| label: qwen-top-projects

if "qwen" in cov["source"].values:
    df = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        project_id,
        project,
        COUNT(*)            AS n_convs,
        MIN(created_at)     AS first_conv,
        MAX(created_at)     AS last_conv
    FROM conversations
    WHERE source = 'qwen' AND project_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY project_id, project
    ORDER BY n_convs DESC
    """).fetchdf()
    if df.empty:
        show_md("No Qwen conversations have a `project_id` populated.")
    else:
        df["first_conv"] = pd.to_datetime(df["first_conv"], utc=True).dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        df["last_conv"]  = pd.to_datetime(df["last_conv"],  utc=True).dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        show_df(df.rename(columns={
            "project_id": "Project ID",
            "project":    "Name (raw)",
            "n_convs":    "Convs",
            "first_conv": "First",
            "last_conv":  "Last",
        }))
```

### Perplexity (spaces)

```{python}
#| label: perplexity-top-projects

if "perplexity" in cov["source"].values:
    df = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        project_id,
        project,
        COUNT(*)            AS n_convs,
        MIN(created_at)     AS first_conv,
        MAX(created_at)     AS last_conv
    FROM conversations
    WHERE source = 'perplexity' AND project_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY project_id, project
    ORDER BY n_convs DESC
    """).fetchdf()
    if df.empty:
        show_md("No Perplexity conversations have a space (`project_id`) populated.")
    else:
        df["first_conv"] = pd.to_datetime(df["first_conv"], utc=True).dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        df["last_conv"]  = pd.to_datetime(df["last_conv"],  utc=True).dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        show_df(df.rename(columns={
            "project_id": "Space ID",
            "project":    "Name (raw)",
            "n_convs":    "Convs",
            "first_conv": "First",
            "last_conv":  "Last",
        }))
```

## 3. CLI working directory (raw `cwd`, no grouping)

For CLIs the platform has no project concept — instead, each session
records the **working directory** where it was launched. The filho stores
that path verbatim in `project`. Path-level fragmentation (different
subfolders of the same repo, or moves across machines) is **expected** and
is *not* normalised here. Grouping into a canonical "project_group" is
analysis work and lives in the consumer project.

```{python}
#| label: cli-fragmentation

cli_df = con.execute("""
SELECT
    source,
    project,
    COUNT(*)        AS n_convs,
    MIN(created_at) AS first_conv,
    MAX(created_at) AS last_conv
FROM conversations
WHERE source IN ('claude_code','codex','gemini_cli')
  AND project IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY source, project
ORDER BY n_convs DESC
""").fetchdf()

if cli_df.empty:
    show_md("No CLI conversations have a `project` (cwd) populated.")
else:
    summary = cli_df.groupby("source").agg(
        unique_paths=("project", "nunique"),
        total_convs =("n_convs", "sum"),
    ).reset_index()
    summary["label"] = summary["source"].map(SOURCE_LABEL)
    summary["avg_convs_per_path"] = (summary["total_convs"] / summary["unique_paths"]).round(1)

    show_df(summary[["label", "unique_paths", "total_convs", "avg_convs_per_path"]].rename(columns={
        "label":               "CLI",
        "unique_paths":        "Unique paths",
        "total_convs":         "Total convs",
        "avg_convs_per_path":  "Avg convs/path",
    }))
```

```{python}
#| label: cli-top-paths

if not cli_df.empty:
    top = cli_df.head(20).copy()
    top["short"] = top["project"].apply(
        lambda p: p.replace("/Users/mosx/Desktop/", "~/Desktop/")
                   .replace("/Users/mosx/", "~/")
                   if isinstance(p, str) else p
    )
    top["label"] = top["source"].map(SOURCE_LABEL)
    show_df(top[["label", "short", "n_convs"]].rename(columns={
        "label":   "CLI",
        "short":   "Path (raw, abbreviated)",
        "n_convs": "Convs",
    }))
```

## 4. Project metadata (`project_metadata.parquet`)

Per-project metadata exported by platforms that ship richer info than just
ID + name (descriptions, system prompts, creation dates). Filho carries
this as a separate canonical table.

```{python}
#| label: project-metadata-coverage

if has_view(con, "project_metadata"):
    pm = con.execute("""
    SELECT source, COUNT(*) AS n_projects
    FROM project_metadata
    GROUP BY source
    ORDER BY n_projects DESC
    """).fetchdf()
    if pm.empty:
        show_md("`project_metadata.parquet` is empty.")
    else:
        pm["label"] = pm["source"].map(SOURCE_LABEL)
        show_df(pm[["label", "n_projects"]].rename(columns={
            "label":     "Source",
            "n_projects":"Projects with metadata",
        }))

    cols = con.execute("DESCRIBE SELECT * FROM project_metadata").fetchdf()
    show_md(f"**Columns available:** `{', '.join(cols['column_name'].tolist())}`")
else:
    show_md("`project_metadata` view not present.")
```

## 5. Gap-by-design vs gap-real

Two different reasons a conversation can be project-less. Mixing them in a
single "orphan" bucket hides the difference and inflates the apparent
problem.

- **Gap-by-design** — platform has no project concept. NULL is the correct
  value. Nothing to fix at the extractor layer.
- **Gap-real** — platform *does* have the concept, but the conversation was
  not assigned to a project (user behaviour, not extractor failure).

```{python}
#| label: gap-decomposition

decomp = con.execute(f"""
SELECT
    source,
    COUNT(*)                                                    AS total,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN project_id IS NULL AND project IS NULL
               THEN 1 END)                                      AS orphans
FROM conversations
GROUP BY source
""").fetchdf()

decomp["bucket"] = decomp["source"].map(
    lambda s: "native" if s in PROJECT_NATIVE
    else "cli" if s in PROJECT_CLI
    else "none-by-design"
)

decomp["gap_type"] = decomp["bucket"].map({
    "none-by-design": "by-design",
    "native":         "real",
    "cli":            "real",
})

decomp["label"] = decomp["source"].map(SOURCE_LABEL)
decomp["pct_orphan"] = (decomp["orphans"] / decomp["total"] * 100).round(1)

# Only show gap-real platforms in the "to investigate" table
real = decomp[decomp["gap_type"] == "real"].sort_values("pct_orphan", ascending=False)
by_design = decomp[decomp["gap_type"] == "by-design"]

show_md("### Gap-real (platform has the concept; some convs unassigned)")
show_df(real[["label", "bucket", "total", "orphans", "pct_orphan"]].rename(columns={
    "label":        "Source",
    "bucket":       "Bucket",
    "total":        "Total",
    "orphans":      "Without project",
    "pct_orphan":   "% orphan",
}))

show_md("### Gap-by-design (platform has no project concept; NULL is correct)")
show_df(by_design[["label", "total"]].rename(columns={
    "label":  "Source",
    "total":  "Total convs (all NULL by design)",
}))
```

```{python}
#| label: gap-stacked

import plotly.graph_objects as go

real_sorted = real.sort_values("orphans", ascending=False)
linked = real_sorted["total"] - real_sorted["orphans"]

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=real_sorted["label"],
    y=linked,
    name="Linked to a project",
    marker_color="#10B981",
    text=linked.apply(fmt_int),
    textposition="inside",
))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=real_sorted["label"],
    y=real_sorted["orphans"],
    name="Orphan (gap-real)",
    marker_color="#F59E0B",
    text=real_sorted["orphans"].apply(fmt_int),
    textposition="inside",
))
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Gap-real only — platforms that expose project concept",
    height=400,
    template="plotly_white",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02),
    xaxis_title=None,
    yaxis_title="conversations",
)
fig.show()
```

## What lives in the consumer

Anything not strictly reported by the platform belongs in the analysis
project, not here:

- Semantic aliasing (e.g. `MOSx` ≡ `Personal`)
- Path grouping (e.g. `/qda` and `/qualia-coding` → same project_group)
- NLP-inferred matches for orphan conversations
- Per-conversation overrides
- Multi-project membership

The consumer's `enriched_projects.parquet` records each canonical mapping
together with provenance (`method` ∈ {`native_project`, `path_mapping`,
`conversation_override`}) so the chain back to the raw fact is auditable.
